--- title: "Jupyter Layout" author: "Jane Doe" date: "March 22nd, 2021" format: html jupyter: python3 --- ::: {.callout-tip} ## Learn more See the full guide on [Figures](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/figures.html) and [Article Layout](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/article-layout.html). ::: ## Layout plots ::: {#c6031cbc .cell layout-ncol='2' execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import matplotlib.pyplot as plt plt.plot([1,23,2,4]) plt.show() plt.plot([8,65,23,90]) plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](layout-jupyter_files/figure-html/cell-2-output-1.png){width=566 height=411} ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](layout-jupyter_files/figure-html/cell-2-output-2.png){width=566 height=411} ::: ::: ## Layout tables ::: {layout-ncol=2} ::: {#e51f5c47 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd d = {'one' : [1., 2., 3., 4.], 'two' : [4., 3., 2., 1.]} df = pd.DataFrame(d) df ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
one two
0 1.0 4.0
1 2.0 3.0
2 3.0 2.0
3 4.0 1.0
``` ::: ::: ::: {#efee76ba .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd d = {'one' : [10., 20., 30., 40.], 'two' : [40., 30., 20., 10.]} df = pd.DataFrame(d) df ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
one two
0 10.0 40.0
1 20.0 30.0
2 30.0 20.0
3 40.0 10.0
``` ::: ::: :::
